response: The business-research problem is whether a strengthened exact MILP improves the computational tractability of the same deterministic production-planning problem. Given demands, setup and production costs, capacities, and initial inventory, the model chooses production quantities, setups, and end-of-period inventory subject to demand balance, capacity, and inventory flow, minimizing setup, production, and inventory cost. Valid inequalities tighten the relaxation and may reduce branch-and-bound search without changing the feasible integer plans or objective. The supplied benchmark result supports the narrow statement that the strengthened formulation solved those instances faster. It does not imply a lower true optimal cost or higher firm profit. A profit pathway would require evidence that saved time changes an operational action—for example, meeting a deadline, enabling more frequent reoptimization, solving a relevant larger instance, or obtaining a better incumbent before release—and that this action affects realized outcomes. No deadline, cadence, scale threshold, or profit link is supplied, so the direct conversion from solver seconds to profit is unsupported.
